title: IMD forecasts heatwave over next three days in north Telangana - The New Indian Express
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
full_text:
HYDERABAD: The India Meteorological Department (IMD), Hyderabad, has forecast heatwave conditions in the northern part of the state over the next three days.
On Wednesday, the highest temperature was recorded in Neelwai of Mancherial district at 41.3°C. In Hyderabad, temperatures touched 40°C at Himayathnagar.
According to the IMD, heatwave conditions are likely at isolated places in Adilabad, Komaram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Mulugu, Bhadradri Kothagudem, and Khammam districts on June 18.
The IMD said light to moderate rain or thundershowers were likely at isolated places across the state during the next two days.
On June 19, light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms is expected in some parts of Telangana, with gusty winds of 30-40 kmph likely at isolated locations.
Meanwhile, the southwest monsoon has advanced further and, as of June 17, its northern limit passes through parts of the Arabian Sea, Maharashtra, Telangana, Odisha, Jharkhand, Bihar, and Nepal.

title: Heatwave grips Telangana as temperatures touch 43°C, monsoon advance likely - Hyderabad Mail
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
full_text:
HYDERABAD: Telangana continued to experience intense heat on Tuesday, with maximum temperatures rising by up to 3°C. Several districts recorded temperatures between 40°C and 43°C.
Neelwai in Mancherial district recorded the state’s highest temperature at 43°C. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) reported heatwave conditions in Adilabad, Komaram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Mulugu and Bhadradri Kothagudem districts.
The IMD said heatwave conditions would likely persist on Wednesday in Adilabad, Komaram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Mulugu and Bhadradri Kothagudem districts.
However, the southwest monsoon is likely to advance further into Telangana over the next four to five days.
The IMD said weather conditions favour the monsoon’s progress into more parts of Telangana, Odisha, Jharkhand, Bihar and Chhattisgarh.
Meteorologists linked the current weather pattern to a trough stretching from a cyclonic circulation over east Uttar Pradesh to south coastal Andhra Pradesh. The system passes through east Madhya Pradesh, east Vidarbha and Telangana.
The IMD forecast light to moderate rain or thundershowers at isolated places on Wednesday. It expects weather conditions to improve further by June 18.
